description: In Go, the Go toolchain version (go.mod go directive) and module mode are part of the contract and changing them changes behavior.
body: In Go, the Go toolchain version (go.mod go directive) and module mode define how code compiles, lints, and resolves dependencies; bumping any of them can change diagnostics and emitted output. Use when you touch the Go toolchain version (go.mod go directive) and module mode or when a build behaves differently than the pinned environment. Do not silently upgrade the toolchain or override the pinned version to make an error disappear. Actions: read the pinned config, match the project's the Go toolchain version (go.mod go directive) and module mode, then run go vet, staticcheck, and the race detector (go test -race) and go build ./... with the module's build tags and GOOS/GOARCH to compare against the baseline. Validation signals include identical go vet, staticcheck, and the race detector (go test -race) output, a reproducible build, and unchanged lockfile resolution. Risk boundary: a toolchain bump is a deliberate, reviewed change; escalate rather than upgrading to unblock yourself.
